completionQuestion.question: Which conclusion is best supported by the three sources in this case?
options: A. Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples all shared exactly the same culture.
B. Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander cultures are diverse, deeply connected to Country, and continue through storytelling.
C. Indigenous culture is mainly important because it is part of the distant past.
completionQuestion.correct: B. Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander cultures are diverse, deeply connected to Country, and continue through storytelling.

completionQuestion.question: Which conclusion is best supported by the three sources in this case?
options: A. Trade between First Nations peoples was rare and mostly accidental.
B. First Nations trade networks were complex, long-distance, and connected people within Australia and beyond.
C. Only European settlement created meaningful trade in Australia.
completionQuestion.correct: B. First Nations trade networks were complex, long-distance, and connected people within Australia and beyond.

completionQuestion.question: Which conclusion is best supported by the three sources in this case?
options: A. Torres Strait Islander culture was not strongly shaped by the sea.
B. Torres Strait Islander people developed a distinct culture connected to sea, navigation, and ceremony.
C. Torres Strait Islander history can be understood only through one symbol.
completionQuestion.correct: B. Torres Strait Islander people developed a distinct culture connected to sea, navigation, and ceremony.
